stemHtml: A Flight Management System (FMS) can provide an Estimated Position Error (EPE) that is often used to check against the Required Navigation Performance (RNP) value of a procedure or airspace. Which of these best describes the EPE?

options.a: A sum of all the navigational errors accumulated over time that may be used as a measure of the performance of the navigation system after a flight.
options.b: A measure of the reasonableness and accuracy of the given flight management position by applying a specific set of rules to the available navigation inputs.
options.c: An average of the navigation errors computed by two or more FMSS when compared against each other and subsequently applied as a correction to improve position accuracy.
options.d: A measure of the accuracy of the flight management system for navigation, and is demonstrated during initial certification of the aircraft.
